test.observation: 83 tests passed in liteclip-core, 11 tests passed in liteclip-replay, 16 doc-tests passed, 1 integration test passed

typecheck.observation: All crates compiled successfully with no type errors

blockingIssues.description: Keyframe counting bug in CPU non-scaler path. Uses 'self.frame_count % gop' instead of 'self.encoder_frame_count % gop' which is used in GPU path. This causes keyframe misalignment in GOP timing.

Keyframe counting bug in all hardware encoders (nvenc.rs:140, amf.rs:158, qsv.rs:203). Uses 'self.frame_count % gop' instead of 'self.encoder_frame_count % gop'. Keyframe timing drifts from configured GOP interval.

No software encoder fallback when hardware encoder unavailable. Returns 'NoHardwareForAuto' error making app unusable on systems without NVENC/AMF/QSV support. Users cannot use the application at all.

Missing NULL pointer validation after av_hwframe_get_buffer. Code sets (*hw_frame).data[0] = gpu_frame.texture.as_raw() without null check. Cross-device path dereferences texture_ptr without validation. Could cause crashes.
Potential panic from .unwrap() on video stream lookup after seek. If stream indices become invalid, this would crash during clip export.
Potential panic from .unwrap() on audio stream lookup after seek. If stream indices become invalid, this would crash during clip export.
Potential panic from .unwrap() chain in scaler validation. Three consecutive .unwrap() calls on scaler.as_ref().unwrap() could crash during thumbnail generation.
Potential panic from .expect("scaler") - could crash during preview frame extraction if scaler initialization silently failed.

nonBlockingIssues.description: QueryPerformanceCounter uses .expect() - quality improvement for defensive coding
Mic initialization uses polling with 2-second timeout - could use blocking channel
Snapshot try_lock can silently skip packets under contention - documented trade-off
Memory accounting uses Ordering::Relaxed - intentional for performance
QSV encoder unrefs after setting hw_device_ctx - cleanup path needs review
use_native_resolution with (0,0) causes encoder init failure
GPU format mismatch silently falls back to CPU encoding
Packet buffer channel size 1024 represents large potential memory (~14GB)
Unsafe FFmpeg packet manipulation needs explicit SAFETY documentation
Move-or-copy lacks atomicity guarantee for final output placement

appliedUpdates.description: Added frame counting semantics (frame_count vs encoder_frame_count), mutex poison recovery pattern, memory ordering strategy, and proactive eviction pattern documentation

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add SAFETY comment convention: All unsafe impl Send blocks must have SAFETY comments explaining thread-safety assumptions. Pattern found missing in encode/ffmpeg/mod.rs:83 and context.rs:39.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: review-encode-module found unsafe impl Send for FfmpegEncoder and D3d11HardwareContext without SAFETY comments. This pattern is partially followed in sdk_export.rs (SAFETY comments at lines 91/109) but inconsistent.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add SAFETY comment convention: All unsafe blocks must have SAFETY comments documenting why the operation is safe. Pattern partially exists but inconsistently applied across modules.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: review-output-module found sdk_ffmpeg_output.rs unsafe blocks at lines 82, 284 lack SAFETY comments while sdk_export.rs has them. Review-encode-module found context.rs:183 unsafe block lacks SAFETY comment.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document mutex poison recovery pattern as project-wide convention: Use unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) for mutex poison recovery to prevent cascade failures.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: review-buffer-module found this pattern consistently used across 16 locations in spmc_ring.rs. Pattern prevents panic propagation when thread panics while holding mutex.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on error handling: Avoid .unwrap() and .expect() in code paths that could fail due to external factors (FFmpeg API calls, stream lookups). Use .with_context() for meaningful error messages.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: review-encode-module and review-output-module found multiple .unwrap() and .expect() calls that could cause panics. These should be replaced with proper error handling using .context() or .with_context().

rejectedObservations.observation: RNNoise backlog cap line number was misreported
rejectedObservations.reason: Minor finding discrepancy - finding content is correct, line reference was off but verified during scrutiny review. No action needed.
While true, this is a code organization suggestion not a blocking issue. Already documented in findings as non-blocking.
Already covered by blocking issue ENC-004 which addresses NULL pointer validation. Adding to library would duplicate finding.
